module_signals.finding: Reported facts (ARR ramp, two blue-chip contracts, net-cash 9/30/25) are high-confidence and primary-sourced; forward deal economics, financing path, and capacity-conversion are undisclosed. Haircut size, do not cap at WATCH.

Genuine exceptional_bottleneck: NBIS is a pure-play on scarce, power-secured AI capacity, re-rating from ex-Yandex orphan to AI platform. Market under-prices a contracted demand book (Microsoft + Meta) and a $7-9B 2026 ARR path.

Privileged, vertically integrated share-taker at the scarce node (contracted power + NVIDIA access + in-house full stack). Durable medium-term, but contested by hyperscalers and rival neoclouds; not a permanent monopoly.

Revenue tripled YoY each quarter; ARR ~$220M -> ~$551M in three quarters; core adj EBITDA turned positive (~19%); roughly net-cash at 9/30/25 with low-coupon long-dated converts. Warning: capex is enormous/front-loaded and the build is unfunded -> capital-markets dependent.

Real kill paths: dilution death-spiral, AI-capex glut/over-build, customer concentration (Microsoft+Meta), capacity-conversion lag, margin reversal. None confirmed at as-of; net-cash balance sheet, sold-out demand. Caps size; no veto. This is an expensive-growth risk, not a false-cheap or structural-decline trap.

~$21B for a contracted ramp toward a $7-9B ARR exit is asymmetric but priced as growth, not a bargain. As-of $83.71 is below the $92.50 Sept-2025 equity print and far below the $138.75 convertible conversion reference. Starter with structured adds, not back-up-the-truck.

starter_zone: <= ~$90 (at/below the $92.50 Sept-2025 equity print) with thesis intact
add_zone: $75-$95 after a clean ARR print on the $7-9B path AND evidence of debt-funded build; up to ~$120 if asset-backed financing closes
trim_or_no_chase_zone: Far above ~$140 (toward/above the $138.75 convertible conversion reference) without commensurate ARR/financing proof
binding_constraint: Financing the ~2.5GW build without ruinous dilution (asset-backed/corporate debt vs. repeated equity/ATM) while contracted power actually converts to ARR; until asset-backed debt is proven at scale, size is capped.
kill_criteria: Core AI-infrastructure ARR decelerates materially or the $7-9B 2026 exit target is walked back
Connected/active power lags the guided path (220MW connected by CYE2025; 800MW-1GW by CYE2026)
A marquee customer (Microsoft or Meta) renegotiates, materially delays, or cancels
Next major financing is equity-heavy below the $92.50 Sept-2025 print, or asset-backed debt fails to materialize
Core adjusted-EBITDA margin reverses back toward break-even
Industry-wide ASP/utilization deterioration signals a capacity glut
runner_dissent: The mechanical pipeline could err two ways: (a) REJECT/WATCH because NBIS is loss-making, optically expensive, and capital-markets-dependent -- the classic exceptional_bottleneck failure of missing the starter; or (b) CORE because the bottleneck narrative and blue-chip contracts are so compelling. I override toward a disciplined STARTER (2%, max 4% pre-confirmation). The contracted demand book and scarce-bottleneck asymmetry justify owning it now; the unfunded ~2.5GW build and dilution risk forbid a high-conviction core until asset-backed financing is demonstrated. Correct control: own small, add on financing + ARR confirmation -- not wait for perfect evidence, not max-size the story.
